destinations.evidence: SPC verification reads fail immediately on a missing job instead of returning a partial map.

equivalence_argument: The SPC reader checks jobExecutor for every requested job and returns an error on the first zero executor, preventing silent omission from the verification map.
notes: This maps only the SPC reader behavior, not settlement reader behavior.
rationale: The primary diff changes the zero-executor branch in read_jobs_for_settlement from continue to Err with a message that non-existent jobs cannot be settled. The primary diff changes the zero-executor branch in read_jobs_for_spc_verification from continue to Err with a message that non-existent jobs cannot be verified for SPC. Collapsed 2 closely related historical candidate statements into the latest effective formulation.
statement: read_jobs_for_spc_verification must return an error when any requested job_id has a zero jobExecutor in AsyncJobTracker, rather than silently omitting that job from the returned map.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call read_jobs_for_settlement with two job IDs where one has valid storage and one has jobExecutor zero. Assert the function returns Err and does not return a partial map omitting the missing job.

Call read_jobs_for_spc_verification with a missing job whose jobExecutor storage slot is zero and assert the function returns Err. Include a mixed valid/missing job set and assert it still fails rather than returning only the valid job.
summary: SPC job reads fail closed for missing jobs.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call read_jobs_for_settlement with two job IDs where one has valid storage and one has jobExecutor zero. Assert the function returns Err and does not return a partial map omitting the missing job.
title: SPC job reads fail closed
